provisions.description: Provides State agencies operating SNAP with options to extend certification periods, adjust periodic reporting requirements, allow simplified recertification via household reporting, and adjust interview requirements through specified dates in 2021, without prior USDA approval. notes: SEC. 4603(a)(1)(A)(i): States may extend certification periods by up to 6 months and adjust periodic reports for households with periods expiring on or before June 30, 2021

SEC. 4603(a)(1)(A)(iii): States may adjust interview requirements through June 30, 2021

provisions.program_name: Medicare Part B Premium and Deductible — 2021 Actuarial Rate Adjustment
provisions.statutory_reference: Section 1839(a) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395r(a))
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text:
Section 1839(a) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395r(a)) is amended— 
(1) in the second sentence of paragraph (1), by striking ''(5) and (6)''
notes: Subsection (a): Limits the 2021 Medicare Part B monthly actuarial rate increase for enrollees age 65+ to 25 percent of the difference between the 2020 rate and the preliminary 2021 rate.
Adds new paragraph (7) to Section 1839(a) establishing a formula that blends the 2020 rate with 25% of the difference to the preliminary 2021 rate.
No explicit dollar amount; the effect is to suppress the premium increase that would otherwise occur, reducing mandatory premium collections and requiring offsetting General Fund transfers.

cross_references.target: Section 1839(a) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395r(a))
cross_references.description: Adds paragraph (7) and conforming amendments to paragraphs (1) and (6)(C)

provisions.raw_text:
Section 1844 of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395w) is amended— 
(1) in subsection (a), by adding at the end the following new sentence: ''In ap
notes: Subsection (b): Amends Section 1844 to add new subsection (e), which requires a General Fund transfer to the SMI Trust Fund equal to the reduction in aggregate premiums attributable to the new rate formula in Section 1839(a)(7).
Also provides for repayment of the transitional contribution through future premium increases under Section 1839(a)(6).
Transfers apply separately for enrollees age 65+ and enrollees under age 65.

provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text:
Section 1844 of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395w), as amended by subsection (b)(2), is amended by adding at the end the following: 

(f) 
(1) Th

provisions.description: Amends Section 286(u) of the Immigration and Nationality Act to expand premium processing authority to additional immigration benefit types (employment-based nonimmigrant petitions, employment-based immigrant petitions, applications to change or extend nonimmigrant status, applications for employment authorization, and others deemed appropriate). Establishes premium fee amounts ($2,500 general; $1,500 for H-2B and R visa petitions), biennial CPI adjustment authority, permissible uses of fees, and premium processing service standards. Also allows the Secretary to set fees for newly designated benefit types without notice-and-comment rulemaking, subject to specified caps and processing timeframes.
